scope.summary: Add `body_friction_override: Option<f32>` to `tailor_avatars` (or `ClothBodyProxy`) so a per-avatar friction multiplier overrides the fabric-level `friction_coefficient` in the cloth-body Coulomb friction pass, mirroring MD's Avatar friction property
acceptance_criteria: `tailor_avatars` gains a `body_friction_override: FLOAT` nullable column (migration); when `NOT NULL` this value is loaded into `ClothBodyProxy.body_friction: Option<f32>` and passed to the collision pass instead of the per-fabric `FabricProperties.friction`
The cloth-body Coulomb friction WGSL shader (used by MT-098) reads `body_friction` from the `GpuCapsule` or `GpuSphere` buffer slots rather than the material UBO when the override is set

An integration test runs the same garment on two avatars with different `body_friction_override` values (0.1 vs 0.7) during an animated walk cycle and asserts the high-friction avatar retains garment position while the low-friction avatar allows measurable sliding
scope.risk_if_missed: All avatars share the same global friction coefficient baked into the fabric preset; garments slide off or cling identically regardless of body surface material (wet skin vs clothing underneath). Cannot match MD's per-avatar friction control for diverse character pipelines.

risks: Broad MT scope can hide multiple review targets.
Markdown MT lists may disagree with packet-declared active/next MT truth.
Receipt routing may target an old MT if contract identity is omitted.
minimum_controls: Exactly one primary proof target per MT unless the packet records why not.
MT identity must be stable and referenced by receipt correlation.
Generated projection drift must not wake Coder or Validator by itself.
